Bash(command="python tools/render/burndown.py --help 2>&1 | head -50", description="Check burndown tool usage")
usage: burndown.py [-h] [--start START] [--end END] [--exclude EXCLUDE]
                   [--port PORT]

Render an issue burndown bar chart to the dashboard

options:
  -h, --help         show this help message and exit
  --start START      Start date YYYY-MM-DD (default: 7 days ago)
  --end END          End date YYYY-MM-DD (default: today)
  --exclude EXCLUDE  Comma-separated issue numbers to exclude (e.g.
                     "48,49,111")
  --port PORT
✅ Check list_issues tool usage · 0.1s

usage: list_issues.py [-h] [--state {open,closed,all}] [--limit LIMIT]
                      [--label LABEL] [--repo REPO]

List GitHub issues

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --state {open,closed,all}
  --limit LIMIT
  --label LABEL
  --repo REPO
The burndown tool defaults to the last 7 days — exactly what you need. Let me run it:
✅ Generate 7-day burndown chart · 1.2s
Bash(command="python tools/render/burndown.py --start 2026-04-27 --end 2026-05-04", description="Generate 7-day burndown chart")

| Closed: 30 | Still Open: 12
